page: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The CPU features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. However, it is premium storage capable, allowing high-performance external block storage attachment. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance is optimized for network throughput, making it suitable for bandwidth-heavy workloads.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-threaded performance, particularly in decompression tasks, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaching 175.85 GB/sec. Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, with read speeds of 202.93 GB/sec and write speeds of 87.93 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is highly efficient at 46.21 GB/sec. Other metrics, such as single-core stress-ng performance and Redis SET operations at 36.28 million operations per second, fall within the average range.

The D32nls_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy and network-intensive tasks that do not require high memory density. By limiting memory to 2.0 GB per core and omitting local storage, the instance reduces resource overhead. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, network appliances, multi-threaded data decompression, and compute-bound tasks utilizing AVX-512 or AMX instructions.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized, low-memory virtual machine from the Dnlv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:2 compute-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium external storage. Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and static web serving throughput, which reaches 46.21 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core CPU performance and database operations align with average tiers. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, network-intensive applications that do not demand high memory capacity, making it suitable for web serving, network appliances, and multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and strong multi-threaded decompression and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A network-optimized, compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced low-memory architecture for high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 64.0 GB of system memory
Optimized for high network throughput and premium external storage
Strong multi-threaded decompression and large-block memory bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated workloads
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for static web serving, network appliances, and compute-bound tasks
